---
title: ""
page-layout: custom
title-block-banner: false
---

```{=html}
<!-- HERO — Cloud sunset sky -->
<section class="hero-section">

  <div class="hero-cloud-overlay"></div>

  <div class="hero-content">

    <h1 class="hero-name">Roo</h1>
    <p class="hero-tagline">soft mind, sharp thinking</p>

    <div class="hero-pills">
      <span class="hero-pill">☁ Fintech</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">⊕ Risk & AML</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">·· Data Stories</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">◈ Tech + Aesthetics</span>
    </div>

    <p class="hero-scroll">scroll to explore ↓</p>
  </div>

</section>

<!-- GRADIENT BRIDGE — bleeds hero colour into content -->
<div class="hero-bridge"></div>

<!-- INTRO -->
<div class="intro-section">
  <div class="intro-inner">
    <p class="intro-eyebrow">Hello</p>
    <h2 class="intro-heading">I'm Roopa — Roo.</h2>
    <div class="intro-rule"></div>
    <p class="intro-body">
      Masters student at SMU, with an interest in
      <em>understanding financial crime and studying the intersection
      of technology, finance, and design</em>.
      I like to build things that are impactful and beautiful —
      data tells many stories; it is a matter of perspective,
      and that is what I like to explore.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- PROJECTS — editorial stacked rows -->
<div class="projects-section">

  <div class="projects-header">
    <p class="projects-eyebrow">Independent Work · Fintech &amp; Financial Crime</p>
    <h2 class="projects-heading">Projects</h2>
    <p class="projects-subhead">
      Self-directed explorations across AML typologies, risk analytics,
      and emerging areas in financial crime — built to go beyond the classroom
      and demonstrate genuine curiosity about where the field is heading.
    </p>
  </div>

  <!-- Project 01 -->
  <a href="projects/art-pep/index.html" class="project-row">
    <div class="project-index">01</div>
    <div class="project-body">
      <div class="project-meta">
        <span class="project-domain">Art Markets · PEP Networks</span>
        <span class="project-tools">R · Tableau · ICIJ · AML</span>
      </div>
      <h3 class="project-title">Art Markets &amp; the PEP Problem</h3>
      <p class="project-desc">
        The art market's opacity makes it a documented vehicle for laundering —
        especially among politically exposed persons. Combining auction data,
        ICIJ offshore leaks, and PEP network analysis to visualise how money
        and influence move through high-value cultural assets.
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="project-arrow">→</div>
  </a>

  <!-- Project 02 -->
  <a href="projects/tbml/index.html" class="project-row">
    <div class="project-index">02</div>
    <div class="project-body">
      <div class="project-meta">
        <span class="project-domain">Trade Finance · Mirror Analysis</span>
        <span class="project-tools">Python · UN Comtrade · FATF</span>
      </div>
      <h3 class="project-title">Trade-Based Money Laundering</h3>
      <p class="project-desc">
        TBML is one of the most significant and least visible ML typologies —
        manipulating trade invoices to move value across borders. Using UN Comtrade
        mirror trade data, this project surfaces discrepancies across high-risk
        corridors and maps them to FATF and MAS Notice 626 frameworks.
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="project-arrow">→</div>
  </a>

  <!-- Project 03 -->
  <a href="projects/crypto/index.html" class="project-row">
    <div class="project-index">03</div>
    <div class="project-body">
      <div class="project-meta">
        <span class="project-domain">On-Chain Analytics · Regulation</span>
        <span class="project-tools">Python · Etherscan · Research</span>
      </div>
      <h3 class="project-title">Crypto &amp; Financial Crime</h3>
      <p class="project-desc">
        An honest exploration of on-chain analytics and the evolving regulatory
        landscape for virtual assets. Part research, part hands-on — tracing
        transaction flows, understanding mixer patterns, and mapping where
        MAS and FATF guidance currently stands.
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="project-arrow">→</div>
  </a>

</div>
```
